channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Hundreds of students, dozens of colleges hit by Trump's visa purge: What to know
item.description: International students are seeing their visas revoked for infractions as minor as traffic violations.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, NewsNation

item.title: Trump reverses Biden rule restricting water usage for showerheads and other home appliances
item.description: President Donald Trump has long complained about modern rules that limit water flow for showerheads, making it harder for him to wash his "beautiful hair.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Daly, WFLA, NBC

item.title: US commander warns that China and North Korea's military aid to Russia creates risks in the Pacific
item.description: The top U.S. commander in the Pacific is warning senators that the military support that China and North Korea are providing to Russia in its war on Ukraine risks security in his region as Moscow provides critical military assistance to both in return.
item.dc:creator: Lolita C. Baldor, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Where things stand for Trump in global tariff battle
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — In the aftermath of this week's tariff whiplash, President Donald Trump is deciding exactly what he wants out of trade talks with as many as 75 nations in the coming weeks. Trump is also figuring out next steps with China.
item.dc:creator: JOSH BOAK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Florida brings charges against a man jailed on federal counts of trying to assassinate Trump
item.description: Florida's attorney general says his office will bring terrorism and attempted murder counts against a man already jailed on federal charges of attempting to assassinate then-presidential candidate Donald Trump while he was golfing last fall.
item.dc:creator: Stephany Matat, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: House votes to overturn Biden-era rule limiting bank overdraft fees to $5, sends to Trump to sign
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The House voted Wednesday to overturn a rule that would have limited bank overdraft fees to $5, following the Senate in moving to dismantle the regulation that the Biden administration had estimated would save consumers billions of dollars.
item.dc:creator: CORA LEWIS and MARY CLARE JALONICK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: New York lawmakers push 'Peanut's Law' to reform wildlife enforcement
item.description: The death of P'Nut, an Instagram-famous squirrel, inspired legislation aimed at reforming wildlife enforcement.
item.dc:creator: Taylor Delandro, NewsNation

item.title: House Republicans pass bill requiring proof of citizenship to vote
item.description: House Republicans for a second time passed a stand-alone bill that would require proof of citizenship to vote in federal elections and impose voter roll purge requirements on states. The legislation — formally titled the Safeguard American Voter Eligibility (SAVE) Act — passed in a 220-208 vote.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: FLASHBACK: Mitt Romney mocked in 2012 for self-deportation concept that has now become a reality
item.description: Former Sen. Mitt Romney was mocked in his 2012 presidential run for promoting the idea that illegal immigrants could be encouraged to self-deport.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Dems fight bill to stop illegal immigrant voting despite polls showing voter support
item.description: The House passed the SAVE Act on noncitizen voting on Thursday, but it still needs to pass the Senate before President Donald Trump can sign it into law.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump's 'big, beautiful' tax agenda scores major victory in House despite GOP rebellion threats
item.description: In a massive victory for the GOP on Thursday, the House passed a bill that will set the stage for a massive conservative policy overhaul.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: 5 takeaways from the Trump tariff drama
item.description: Financial markets soared on Wednesday after President Trump issued a 90-day pause in almost all the tariffs that had roiled the global economy during the past week.
item.dc:creator: Niall Stanage, NewsNation

item.title: Boozy battle: Maryland and Delaware fight over 'Orange Crush' cocktail
item.description: Lawmakers in Maryland voted Monday to approve the orange crush as the Old Line state's official state cocktail.
item.dc:creator: Judy Kurtz, NewsNation

item.title: Rep. Ilhan Omar will run for reelection in the U.S. House, not an open Minnesota Senate seat
item.description: U.S. Rep. Ilhan Omar had been considering a run for the seat currently held by Sen. Tina Smith. The DFLer will instead seek a fifth House term.
item.dc:creator: Clay Masters, MPR News

item.title: Public bathroom access in NYC to be voted on by City Council
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – Knowing where the closest bathroom is in New York City can be a challenge, but a new City Council bill may change that. Councilmember Sandy Nurse is leading the push to expand public bathroom access.
item.dc:creator: Erin Pflaumer, PIX 11

item.title: Johnson scraps vote on Trump budget blueprint in face of conservative opposition
item.description: House Republican leaders on Wednesday scrapped plans to vote on the Senate’s framework to advance key parts of President Trump’s legislative agenda, a major setback that came in the face of opposition from hard-line conservatives. Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, NewsNation

item.title: Trump reverses Biden rule restricting water usage for showerheads and other home appliances
item.description: President Donald Trump has long complained about modern rules that limit water flow for showerheads, making it harder for him to wash his “beautiful hair.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Daly, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Options for Trump's space-based 'Golden Dome' missile defense shield head to Hegseth for approval
item.description: COLORADO SPRINGS, Colo. (AP) — U.S. Space Command said Wednesday that it has finalized options for President Donald Trump's “Golden Dome” missile defense system and has sent its recommendations to Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth for his review and approval.
item.dc:creator: TARA COPP, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: U of M president outlines efforts to combat antisemitism in wake of White House investigation
item.description: University of Minnesota President Rebecca Cunningham sent a letter to faculty, students and staff underscoring her commitment to preventing antisemitism. A key regional Jewish advocacy organization says the school is making the right moves.
item.dc:creator: Mark Zdechlik, MPR News

item.title: Lawmakers cut 6 new positions in Nevada Department of Public Safety budget request
item.description: Investigation backlogs and training needs within the Nevada Department of Public Safety are among the justifications for new positions in the state budget, but lawmakers said Wednesday there's just not enough money to pay for everything.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Cortez Masto proposes tax relief for working class Americans
item.description: LAS VEGAS (KLAS) -- Democratic Nevada Sen. Catherine Cortez Masto and other colleagues on Wednesday introduced a new bill in Washington, D.C., aimed at putting more money back in the pockets of working Americans. Democratic Nevada Sen.
item.dc:creator: Madina Ansary, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: U.S. says it is now monitoring immigrants' social media for antisemitism
item.description: Effective immediately, the government says it will begin screening immigrant social media for activity that officials think indicates support for antisemitism.
item.dc:creator: Jasmine Garsd, MPR News

item.title: Real ID rules take effect in May; Minnesota officials say it’s time to apply
item.description: Starting May 7, travelers will need a Real ID or approved alternative to get through airport security. Officials say about 40 percent of Minnesotans have a Real ID. They’re encouraging everyone to get the necessary travel documents ahead of the deadline.
item.dc:creator: Estelle Timar-Wilcox, MPR News

item.title: ICE to open office on Rikers Island with Mayor Eric Adams' permission
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – ICE is allowed to open an office inside New York City’s biggest jail, according to an executive order signed by Deputy Mayor Randy Mastro. The order stated that the safety of the city has been jeopardized by gangs like MS-13 and Tren de Aragua.
item.dc:creator: Kala Rama, PIX 11

item.title: Companies need 'time machine' for tariffs to make sense: Andrew Yang
item.description: "Instead of reshoring manufacturing, [CEOs] are just cutting investment, they're laying people off," Yang said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Dorn, NewsNation

item.title: AP wins reinstatement to White House events after judge rules government can’t bar its journalists
item.description: A federal judge ordered the White House on Tuesday to restore The Associated Press’ full access to cover presidential events, affirming on First Amendment grounds that the government cannot punish the news organization for the content of its speech. U.S. District Judge Trevor N.
item.dc:creator: DAVID BAUDER, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Amy Coney Barrett is what is needed in Supreme Court justice: Analyst
item.description: Barrett has sided with liberal justices on multiple hot-button issues, including DOGE regulation and Arizona’s voter ID law.
item.dc:creator: Jesse Weber, NewsNation

item.title: Sen. Jim Justice 'really proud' of Trump for coal executive orders
item.description: The White House said the orders were enacted to "secure America’s economic prosperity and national security.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: Lombardo's crime bill focuses on harsher penalties in Nevada, penalizing repeat offenders
item.description: Republican Nevada Gov. Joe Lombardo is proposing harsher penalties for repeat offenders, DUI drivers and other criminals as part of his sweeping crime bill introduced Monday.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Senate confirms Trump’s controversial pick for Pentagon No. 3 job
item.description: The Senate voted Tuesday to confirm Elbridge Colby, President Trump’s “lightning rod” pick to serve as the Pentagon’s under secretary for policy, despite the private concerns of several Republican senators about Colby’s past statements and views.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, NewsNation

item.title: Nevada Democrat introduces proposal to ban book bans
item.description: A proposal in the Nevada Legislature would ban school districts and staff from banning books without a court order determining the material is obscene.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump says he's not backing down, calls tariffs 'medicine' as markets reel
item.description: Speaking to reporters aboard Air Force One, Trump said he didn't want global markets to fall, but also that he wasn't concerned about the massive sell-off.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Tampa Bay lawmaker proposes additional Kratom regulations; advocates partially agree
item.description: State lawmakers are looking to add more regulations to the controversial herbal supplement known as Kratom.
item.dc:creator: Ty Russell, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Staff working on childhood lead exposure and cancer clusters fired from CDC
item.description: Staff members who fought childhood lead exposure and those who worked on cancer clusters were among those fired from the Department of Health and Human Services.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Frazin, Fox 5
